title: Cum să nu schimbi un regim politic
summary: Francis Fukuyama criticizes Trump's military intervention against Iran, arguing it repeats the catastrophic mistakes of past US regime change efforts in Afghanistan, Iraq, and Libya. He contends that airstrikes alone cannot produce political change and that the administration's ignorance of regional expertise and reliance on loyalists has led to dangerous improvisation.
talking_points: Trump's strike on Iran was poorly planned, assuming the regime would collapse quickly like Venezuela, but Iran's retaliatory capacity was vastly underestimated
The US failed to learn from regime change disasters in Afghanistan, Iraq, and Libya — military force alone cannot create functioning states or favorable political outcomes
Iran's opposition is deeply fragmented with no organized democratic leadership, and the country is ethnically divided, making regime replacement extraordinarily difficult
The administration has marginalized diplomats, intelligence analysts, and military officers, relying instead on sycophantic loyalists incapable of providing realistic assessments
US bombing of civilian infrastructure like power grids and desalination plants directly contradicts the stated goal of supporting the Iranian people
author: Francis Fukuyama
